perturbation.original_text: 1.3 MERCHANT CONTRACTS. NPC, as agent of NCB, shall be responsible for establishing the terms and conditions of the Merchant Contracts, including all changes thereunder, subject to Section 1.7 below. Without in any way limiting the authority granted in Section 1.1, NCB hereby authorizes NPC to use NCB's name to the extent necessary or appropriate in accordance with the terms of this Agreement to enter into and to renew Merchant Contracts, to modify Merchant Contracts to the extent necessary to assign them to NCB, to perform the Merchant Contracts and to take other necessary or appropriate actions with respect to the Merchant Contracts, all in accordance with requirements of Visa and MasterCard. As between NPC and NCB, NPC shall be responsible for all credit, fraud and other risks associated with each Merchant Contract.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 MERCHANT CONTRACTS. NPC, as agent of NCB, shall be responsible for establishing the terms and conditions of the Merchant Contracts, including all changes thereunder. Without in any way limiting the authority granted in Section 1.1, NCB hereby authorizes NPC to use NCB's name to the extent necessary or appropriate in accordance with the terms of this Agreement to enter into and to renew Merchant Contracts, to modify Merchant Contracts to the extent necessary to assign them to NCB, to perform the Merchant Contracts and to take other necessary or appropriate actions with respect to the Merchant Contracts, all in accordance with requirements of Visa and MasterCard. As between NPC and NCB, NPC shall be responsible for all credit, fraud and other risks associated with each Merchant Contract.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'subject to Section 1.7 below', we are creating an in-text contradiction because Section 1.7 outlines due diligence requirements and risk standards that NPC must adhere to. By removing this clause, it implies NPC has complete autonomy over Merchant Contracts, which contradicts the due diligence obligations described later in Section 1.7. Section 1.7(b) requires NPC to keep NCB's senior management informed of any material changes in the credit and financial review procedures and standards.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.7 DUE DILIGENCE BY NCB; NPC'S STANDARDS. (a) DUE DILIGENCE PRIOR TO EXECUTION OF AGREEMENT. Senior management officials of NPC have met with senior management officials of NCB and have discussed with the NCB officials the credit and financial review procedures and standards used by NPC in deciding whether to accept or retain Merchants as customers for Merchant Processing Services, as well as NPC's experience with respect to any losses resulting from financial failures or fraud by its merchant customers. Schedule A attached hereto outlines the credit and financial review procedures and standards currently used by NPC. NPC represents 2 3 to NCB, and has provided NCB's senior management with financial information showing, that for 1994 and 1995 combined, the losses incurred by NPC as a result of financial failures or fraud by its merchant customers have averaged less than .60% of NPC's net revenues from Merchant Processing Services. (b) CONTINUING DUE DILIGENCE: NPC'S RISK STANDARDS. NPC shall keep NCB's senior management advised of any material changes in the credit and financial review procedures and standards and of any material exceptions to such procedures and standards which may from time to time be made with respect to particular Merchants. NPC shall not make any such changes or exceptions until its management committee has approved such changes or exceptions after carefully evaluating the relative advantages and disadvantages expected to result from such changes or exceptions. NPC also shall continue to advise senior management officials of NCB on a timely basis with respect to any substantial loss (meaning any loss of $1,000,000 or more with respect to a single Merchant or $3,000,000 or more in the aggregate in any twelve month period) incurred by NPC as a result of any financial failures or fraud by its merchant customers. If NCB's senior management officials reasonably determine from time to time that any changes are needed in NPC's credit and financial review procedures and standards or in the implementation thereof in order to avoid any significant increase in NPC's losses from financial failures or fraud by its merchant customers, then NCB shall so notify NPC in writing, and NPC and NCB shall mutually agree upon, and NPC shall implement appropriate changes. (c) DUE DILIGENCE BY NCB FOR NPC PRICING STANDARDS. Senior management officials of NPC have met with senior management officials of NCB and have discussed with the NCB officials the pricing procedures and standards used for Merchants as customers for Merchant Processing Services. NPC shall keep NCB's senior management advised of any material changes in its pricing procedures and standards and of any material exceptions to such procedures and standards which may from time to time be made with respect to particular Merchants. NPC shall not make any such changes or exceptions until its management committee has approved such changes or exceptions after carefully evaluating the relative advantages and disadvantages expected to result from such changes or exceptions. If NCB's senior management officials reasonably determine from time to time that any changes are needed in NPC's pricing procedures and standards or in the implementation thereof, then NCB shall so notify NPC in writing, and NPC and NCB shall mutually agree upon, and NPC shall implement appropriate changes.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 1.3 stated that the terms and conditions of the Merchant Contracts, including all changes, were 'subject to Section 1.7 below'. Section 1.7 outlines due diligence requirements and risk standards that NPC must adhere to. The changed text in Section 1.3 removes this phrase, granting NPC more autonomy over Merchant Contracts. This contradicts Section 1.7, which imposes ongoing due diligence and risk management responsibilities on NPC regarding merchant contracts. Specifically, Section 1.7(b) requires NPC to keep NCB's senior management informed of material changes in credit and financial review procedures and standards. By removing the 'subject to Section 1.7 below' clause, Section 1.3 implies that NPC's authority is not limited by these due diligence requirements, creating a direct conflict with the obligations imposed by Section 1.7.

perturbation.original_text: 1.6 ACCOUNT SETTLEMENT. (a) PAYMENT INSTRUCTIONS. NPC shall prepare and transmit to NCB, in a mutually acceptable format, instructions specifying the payments to be made from the NCB Account to the Merchants and to NPC pursuant to this Agreement (the "Payment Instructions"). Payment Instructions shall be delivered to NCB in accordance with the schedule and procedures established from time to time by the parties. (b) PAYMENT. In accordance with the Payment Instructions, NCB shall pay the Merchants on a timely basis the net settlement amounts due to them and shall credit NPC's account at NCB for the fees due to NPC hereunder. Such payments to Merchants shall be effected through mutually acceptable procedures which are consistent with the payment procedures established pursuant to the Merchant Contracts. NPC shall perform on NCB's behalf all of the administrative and bookkeeping functions necessary to effect payment in such manner.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.6 ACCOUNT SETTLEMENT. (a) PAYMENT INSTRUCTIONS. NPC shall prepare and transmit to NCB, in a mutually acceptable format, instructions specifying the payments to be made from the NCB Account to the Merchants and to NPC pursuant to this Agreement (the "Payment Instructions"). (b) PAYMENT. In accordance with the Payment Instructions, NCB shall pay the Merchants the net settlement amounts due to them and shall credit NPC's account at NCB for the fees due to NPC hereunder. Such payments to Merchants shall be effected through mutually acceptable procedures which are consistent with the payment procedures established pursuant to the Merchant Contracts. NPC shall perform on NCB's behalf all of the administrative and bookkeeping functions necessary to effect payment in such manner.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the sentence 'Payment Instructions shall be delivered to NCB in accordance with the schedule and procedures established from time to time by the parties' from Section 1.6(a), and leaving the reference to Payment Instructions in section 1.6(b), we introduce an in-text contradiction concerning the schedule and procedures for payment. The contradiction arises because section 1.6(b) states that NCB shall pay the merchants 'in accordance with the Payment Instructions,' but without the omitted sentence, there is no longer a defined schedule or procedure for how those Payment Instructions are delivered, creating uncertainty about when and how the payments should be made.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) PAYMENT. In accordance with the Payment Instructions, NCB shall pay the Merchants the net settlement amounts due to them and shall credit NPC's account at NCB for the fees due to NPC hereunder. Such payments to Merchants shall be effected through mutually acceptable procedures which are consistent with the payment procedures established pursuant to the Merchant Contracts. NPC shall perform on NCB's behalf all of the administrative and bookkeeping functions necessary to effect payment in such manner.
perturbation.justification: The omission of 'Payment Instructions shall be delivered to NCB in accordance with the schedule and procedures established from time to time by the parties' from Section 1.6(a) directly contradicts the requirement in Section 1.6(b) (which is also the contradicted text) that 'NCB shall pay the Merchants in accordance with the Payment Instructions.' Without a defined schedule and procedure, it's unclear how NCB can comply with the instructions, creating a direct conflict and making the payment obligation uncertain and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: NPC shall bear all expenses of maintaining facilities and connections necessary to provide Merchant Processing Services except for the facilities and connection maintained by NCB for purposes of effecting payments pursuant to Section 1.6(b). In addition, NPC agrees to pay or reimburse NCB in full all interchange or issuer reimbursement fees on outgoing merchant sales volume, as well as all fee assessments or charges imposed on NCB by Visa or MasterCard as a result of the Merchant Processing Services performed by NPC. Such fees shall be paid by NPC directly when due or shall be paid by NPC to NCB on the banking day immediately prior to the day on which NCB must pay such fees. All such fees, assessments and charges for which NCB seeks payment by NPC shall, upon request, be documented to NPC's reasonable satisfaction as being attributable to NPC's Merchant Processing Services.
perturbation.changed_text: NPC shall bear all expenses of maintaining facilities and connections necessary to provide Merchant Processing Services. In addition, NPC agrees to pay or reimburse NCB in full all interchange or issuer reimbursement fees on outgoing merchant sales volume, as well as all fee assessments or charges imposed on NCB by Visa or MasterCard as a result of the Merchant Processing Services performed by NPC. Such fees shall be paid by NPC directly when due or shall be paid by NPC to NCB on the banking day immediately prior to the day on which NCB must pay such fees. All such fees, assessments and charges for which NCB seeks payment by NPC shall, upon request, be documented to NPC's reasonable satisfaction as being attributable to NPC's Merchant Processing Services.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the phrase 'except for the facilities and connection maintained by NCB for purposes of effecting payments pursuant to Section 1.6(b)' from Article 4.2, we create an in-text contradiction because NCB's responsibilities regarding facilities and connections for payments are no longer clear. This omission is related to Section 1.6, which deals with account settlement, so removing this phrase contradicts the expectations set in Section 1.6(b) where NCB makes payments based on the Payment Instructions, implying NCB has a defined role and costs with that payment processing. This omission introduces ambiguity and creates an enforcement uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) PAYMENT. In accordance with the Payment Instructions, NCB shall pay the Merchants the net settlement amounts due to them and shall credit NPC's account at NCB for the fees due to NPC hereunder. Such payments to Merchants shall be effected through mutually acceptable procedures which are consistent with the payment procedures established pursuant to the Merchant Contracts. NPC shall perform on NCB's behalf all of the administrative and bookkeeping functions necessary to effect payment in such manner.
perturbation.justification: The omission of 'except for the facilities and connection maintained by NCB for purposes of effecting payments pursuant to Section 1.6(b)' from Article 4.2 creates a contradiction with Section 1.6(b). Section 1.6(b) states that 'NCB shall pay the Merchants the net settlement amounts due to them'. The original text in Article 4.2 clarifies the division of expenses, but the changed text makes NPC responsible for all expenses related to Merchant Processing Services, thus contradicting NCB's obligation outlined in 1.6(b), where they make payments and incur costs related to that activity.
